title: decision_audit_record.v1
description: Semi-autonomous pipeline — append-only audit of ONE objective decision the carteiro (auto_decider) drained, OR of a borderline it escalated to the gate (mode B). The RAIL: `decided_by` is NEVER a human. Objective and taste never cross — a taste/aesthetic item is classified TASTE_REFUSED and the machine never touches it (no approve/reject). Deterministic: created_at is derived (artifact mtime), never wall-clock/random. No additionalProperties:false — consumers (FP curadoria/RAG) may enrich.

decision_id.description: identity of the proposal decided (= proposals.state() id, e.g. furniture_program_r004, gap_capacidade_r004)

decision_type.description: RAW proposal type — distinguishes the two decision surfaces

classification.description: the objective judge's verdict class. TASTE_REFUSED = gosto/estético; the machine abstains. INVALID = malformed/irrecoverable.

action.description: what actually happened to the proposal

confidence.description: scalar objective confidence in the classification (derived from the PASS/WARN/FAIL counts of the checks that RAN, scaled by coverage). SKIPPED checks are excluded, never fabricated.

evidence.description: human-readable deterministic evidence lines (intern findings, gate fails/warns, gap title/detail)

judge_verdicts.description: per-checker verdicts fused into the classification (e.g. {interns: PASS, geometry_sanity: PASS|SKIPPED, furniture_overlap: WARN})

gate.description: null unless the item was escalated to ask_gpt_gate (mode B). When present: {trigger, status, verdict, confidence, applied}.

decided_by.description: WHO made the call — the RAIL: never a human. auto_decider = the objective carteiro; gate_mode_b = the delegated oracle gate.

caps_snapshot.description: the job caps in force for this drain (max_auto_decisions_per_drain, max_gate_calls_per_drain, ...) — provenance for why an item may have been left_pending (capped).

corpus_version.description: RAG corpus version in force (provenance); 'unknown' when the RAG index is not wired/reachable.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC, DERIVED from the proposal artifact mtime — never the wall-clock of the decision, never random.

dry_run.description: true = the carteiro only SIMULATED this decision (drain dry_run): the record is written for the dashboard history but NO proposal was moved and NO DLQ row was written — it is what the carteiro WOULD do. false = an --apply drain that actually moved the proposal (or wrote the DLQ). Dedup identity is (decision_id, action, dry_run), so a simulated and an applied record of the same decision coexist.
